macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

items.title: Kevin Warsh takes over the Federal Reserve as inflation heats up
items.summary: Kevin Warsh assumed leadership of the Federal Reserve after Senate confirmation, immediately facing renewed debate over interest rates as inflation has accelerated to a three-year high. The appointment also raises questions about the Fed’s independence, though Warsh says monetary policy will remain insulated from political pressure.

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

items.title: Subnautica 2 reaches 2 million sales in 12 hours
items.summary: VGC reports that Subnautica 2 sold 2 million copies within 12 hours of launch and peaked at more than 600,000 concurrent players, making it one of the biggest recent gaming launches. The strong debut comes despite the game’s troubled development cycle.

items.title: Lego 2K Drive is being delisted in four days
items.summary: VGC says players have only a few days left to buy Lego 2K Drive before it is removed from sale, marking a delisting three years after release. This is a notable reminder of how quickly licensed games can disappear from storefronts.

items.title: Nintendo adds more Virtual Boy games to Switch Online
items.summary: According to VGC, Nintendo’s latest update has already brought most of the Virtual Boy library to Switch Online, with five new titles added. The expansion is one of the more unusual retro-platform updates in recent memory.

items.title: Two unannounced Xbox controllers appear online, including Elite Series 3 and a cloud gaming pad
items.summary: VGC reports that two unrevealed Xbox controllers have surfaced, including what appears to be an Elite Series 3 and a dedicated cloud gaming controller. The leak suggests Microsoft may be preparing new hardware accessories focused on premium play and streaming.

items.title: Best Buy affiliate email sparks GTA 6 preorder speculation
items.summary: GamingBolt reports that Best Buy told affiliates that GTA 6 preorders may begin next week, fueling speculation that Rockstar could soon share new information. The timing has led fans to expect a possible trailer or marketing update.

items.title: Capcom says it sold more games than ever before in its history
items.summary: VGC reports that Capcom posted record-breaking game sales, with Resident Evil cited as a major driver of the company’s results. The milestone underscores the continued strength of Capcom’s key franchises.

items.title: Assassin’s Creed Black Flag Resynced gets half-million-dollar treasure hunt
items.summary: VGC says Ubisoft is launching a real-world treasure hunt tied to Assassin’s Creed Black Flag Resynced, with a prize pool worth half a million dollars. The event is expected to run for years and begins later in 2026.

items.title: Developer apologizes after asking Steam reviewer to delete a negative review
items.summary: VGC reports that Outbound’s developer apologized after asking a player to remove a negative Steam review, but the request backfired and led to more criticism. The incident has drawn attention to how studios handle user feedback publicly.

items.title: Japanese man arrested over threat against Nintendo headquarters
items.summary: VGC says a Japanese man was arrested after allegedly threatening to blow up Nintendo’s headquarters, an incident that echoes similar threats that previously forced Nintendo to cancel live events. The case highlights ongoing security concerns for major game companies.

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.

items.title: Amazon brings Alexa+ to the web
items.summary: Amazon is expanding Alexa+ beyond devices by launching it on the web, signaling a push toward more agentic, browser-based assistant workflows. The move intensifies competition with ChatGPT and Google Gemini.

items.title: Gartner urges enterprises to avoid AI browsers for now
items.summary: Gartner is warning organizations to block AI browsers because agentic browsing tools may expose sensitive data and weaken security controls. The guidance reflects rising enterprise concerns about security risks from new browser integrations.

items.title: Google and Qualcomm push Android PCs as a new computing platform
items.summary: Google and Qualcomm are working to bring Android PCs into the mainstream, aiming to challenge Microsoft and Apple in personal computing. The effort is part of a broader push to unify mobile and desktop experiences.

items.title: Quantum Motion unveils first full-stack quantum computer made with silicon chip fabrication
items.summary: Quantum Motion has deployed the first full-stack quantum computer built using standard silicon fabrication methods. The milestone could improve the scalability of quantum systems.

items.title: Meta brings third-party apps and games to display glasses
items.summary: Meta is adding third-party apps and games to its display glasses, making the $800 device more capable and potentially more appealing to consumers. The update could broaden the glasses’ usefulness beyond basic display functions.

items.title: AT&T, T-Mobile and Verizon team up to reduce coverage dead zones
items.summary: The major U.S. carriers are joining forces to improve satellite capacity and expand connectivity in underserved areas. The collaboration could help eliminate mobile coverage gaps in more remote locations.

items.title: Tesla robotaxi crash data underscores slow rollout progress
items.summary: New crash data shows Tesla robotaxis have had two crashes since July 2025, both involving remote operators. The report adds scrutiny to the pace and safety of Tesla's autonomous ride service.

items.title: Apple reportedly testing Intel 18A-P process for future chips
items.summary: Apple is said to be evaluating Intel's 18A-P process for making chips for iPhone and Mac systems. The initial production run would reportedly focus on older Apple Silicon designs.

items.title: OpenAI launches personal finance tools for ChatGPT Pro users
items.summary: OpenAI has introduced personal finance tools in preview for U.S. ChatGPT Pro subscribers, with Plaid integration providing access to thousands of financial institutions. The feature lets users analyze spending and related financial activity.

items.title: Global semiconductor stocks fall after Trump-Xi summit ends without chip deal
items.summary: Semiconductor shares declined worldwide after the Trump-Xi summit concluded without major chip agreements, and Nvidia and AMD both fell sharply. The market reaction reflects continued uncertainty over U.S.-China chip policy.
